title: Cleavage of the Fusion Protein (TEV Protease)
author: New England Biolabs
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.bfd9ji96

steps.title: If necessary, concentrate the fusion protein to at least 0
steps.instruction: If necessary, concentrate the fusion protein to at least 0.5 milligram per milliliter (mg/mL) .

steps.title: Perform a pilot experiment with a...
steps.instruction: Perform a pilot experiment with a small portion of your protein. For example:

steps.title: Scale up the pilot experiment linearly...
steps.instruction: Scale up the pilot experiment linearly for the amount of the fusion protein to be cleaved. Save at least a small sample of the uncut fusion as a negative control.

steps.title: Check for complete cleavage by SDS-PAGE
steps.instruction: Check for complete cleavage by SDS-PAGE.

steps.title: TEV Protease and the cleaved MBP...
steps.instruction: TEV Protease and the cleaved MBP contain polyhistidine tags at their N-termini. They can be removed from the cleavage reaction by immobilized metal affinity chromatography, such as Nickel or Cobalt resin, thereby isolating the target protein in the flow through.

steps.title: Combine 15 µg, fusion protein and...
steps.instruction: Combine 15 µg, fusion protein and H2O to make a total reaction volume of 45 μl .

steps.title: Add 5 µL, TEV Protease Reaction...
steps.instruction: Add 5 µL, TEV Protease Reaction Buffer (10X) to make a 50 μl total reaction volume .

steps.title: In a separate tube, combine 5...
steps.instruction: In a separate tube, combine 5 µg, fusion protein , 5 µL, TEV Protease Reaction Buffer (10X) and H2O to a volume of 50 μl. Do not add TEV Protease (control sample).

steps.title: Incubate reaction and control sample for...
steps.instruction: Incubate reaction and control sample for 01:00:00 , 03:00:00 , and 08:00:00 at 30 °C (an additional reaction can be made and incubated for 24 hours at 4°C).

steps.title: Take 10 µL, of reaction(s) at...
steps.instruction: Take 10 µL, of reaction(s) at the indicated times above and add 5 µL, SDS-PAGE Sample Buffer (3X) . Take 10 µL, control sample and add 5 µL, SDS-PAGE Sample Buffer (3X) after 8 hours (or longest incubation time).

steps.title: Incubate the SDS-PAGE samples for 00:03:00...
steps.instruction: Incubate the SDS-PAGE samples for 00:03:00 - 00:05:00 at 70 °C - 100 °C .
